Scenario.overview: Energy Sector Complex The Unitronics Defacement Campaign targets the Energy sector with a multi-stage complex cyber attack aimed at gaining unauthorized access, maintaining persistence, escalating privileges, evading defenses, accessing credentials, discovering network resources, moving laterally, collecting sensitive data, exfiltrating information, and impacting operations by defacing systems.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, Command & Control Server, User Account, Public IP/Network Block, File, Target System Artifact
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Email, Web, Network Traffic Flow, Response Metadata, Bash Script, Web Shell, Application Log Content, User Account Authentication, Network Service Scanning, Application Log Content, File Access, Exfiltration Over C2 Channel, Network Traffic Content

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 309 Name: Network Topology Mapping
stage.description: The attackers engage in active scanning activities to map network nodes, hosts, devices, and routes within the energy sector's external network.

stage.name: Resource Development
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 522 Name: Malicious Hardware Component Replacement
stage.description: The attackers acquire infrastructure such as phishing email servers and domains mimicking legitimate entities to support their operation.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: Attackers send crafted emails to employees with malicious attachments or links to gain initial access to the energy sector's systems.

stage.name: Execution
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 88 Name: OS Command Injection
stage.description: After initial access, attackers execute malware via command line using bash scripts.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 650 Name: Upload a Web Shell to a Web Server
stage.description: To maintain access, attackers deploy a web shell on compromised web servers to establish a persistent backdoor.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 121 Name: Exploit Non-Production Interfaces
stage.description: Attackers exploit unpatched software vulnerabilities to escalate privileges within the compromised environment.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 578 Name: Disable Security Software
stage.description: Attackers impair detection efforts by disabling antivirus and other monitoring tools to evade defenses.

stage.name: Credential Access
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 49 Name: Password Brute Forcing
stage.description: Attackers attempt to gain valid credentials through repeated brute-force attacks on user accounts.

stage.name: Discovery
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 309 Name: Network Topology Mapping
stage.description: Attackers map out network infrastructure to identify valuable systems and resources for further exploitation.

stage.name: Lateral Movement
stage.description: Using stolen credentials, attackers move laterally across the network to expand their foothold.

stage.name: Collection
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 328 Name: TCP 'RST' Flag Checksum Probe
stage.description: Attackers gather sensitive data from compromised systems by accessing local files.

stage.name: Exfiltration
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 24 Name: Filter Failure through Buffer Overflow
stage.description: Collected data is transferred to attacker-controlled servers over a command and control channel.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 675 Name: Retrieve Data from Decommissioned Devices
stage.description: Attackers display unwanted visual content on systems to disrupt operations and assert control.

Scenario.summary: The Unitronics Defacement Campaign employs a sophisticated approach targeting the energy sector, utilizing phishing for initial access, followed by multiple stages of persistence, privilege escalation, defense evasion, credential access, discovery, lateral movement, collection, exfiltration, and impact through defacement, aiming to disrupt operations and steal sensitive data.
